message.content:
Explore the editor tools and user interaction patterns in this modular apartment planner. I need to understand:

1. Read src/editor/tools.js - tool definitions
2. Read src/editor/useEditorTool.js - how tools are used
3. Read src/editor/handlers/selectHandler.js
4. Read src/app/EditorProvider.jsx - editor state management
5. Look for any tooltip, popover, or contextual UI components
6. Check for any notification or feedback UI components
7. Find any keyboard shortcut handling or accessibility features

Report the full contents so I can understand the complete interaction model - how users switch tools, get feedback, and navigate the interface.

content.text: I'll explore the editor tools and user interaction patterns by reading the specified files and searching for UI feedback components.

content.text: Let me read the SvgCanvas to see the status message display:
Perfect! Now let me get more of the SvgCanvas to see the full structure and check keyboard handling:

Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me compile the final report: ## Complete Editor Tools & User Interaction Model Report Based on my exploration of the codebase, here's the full picture of how editor tools and user interactions work in this modular apartment planner: ### 1. **Tool Definitions** (`src/editor/tools.js`) The application defines 17 tools: - **SELECT** - Selection/manipulation - **DIMENSION** - Add measurements - **WALL** - Draw walls - **BEAM** - Add structural beams - **STAIR** - Add stairs - **SECTION** - Create section cuts - **SLAB** - Add floor slabs - **ROOM** - Define room boundaries - **DOOR** - Place doors - **WINDOW** - Place windows - **COLUMN** - Add columns - **LANDING** - Add landings - **FIXTURE** - Place fixtures (kitchen tops, toilets, furniture) - **RAILING** - Add railings - **TRUSS_DRAW** - Draw trusses - **ROOF_PARAPET** - Add roof parapets - **ROOF_DRAIN** - Add roof drains - **ROOF_OPENING** - Add roof openings Fixtures have 7 subtypes: Kitchen Top, Toilet, Lavatory, Table, TV, Sofa, Bed ### 2. **Tool Instantiation** (`src/editor/useEditorTool.js`) The `useEditorTool` hook is the central dispatcher that creates tool handlers based on: - Current `activeTool` - `modelTarget` (floor/roof/truss) - `viewMode` (plan/elevation/section) - Context needed by each handler (dispatch, editor state, floor data, viewport, snap settings) Key pattern: Read-only mode is enforced when not in plan view for floor/roof/truss models via `createReadOnlyHandler()`. ### 3. **Tool Handler Interface** (implemented by each handler) All handlers follow this contract: ```javascript { onMouseDown(modelPos, e, toolState), onMouseMove(modelPos, e, toolState, selectedId, selectedType), onMouseUp(modelPos, e, toolState), onDoubleClick(modelPos, e, toolState), onKeyDown(e, toolState, selectedId, selectedType), getCursor(toolState) -> cursor style string } ``` ### 4. **Select Handler** (`src/editor/handlers/selectHandler.js`) The select tool implements sophisticated interaction: **Hit Testing Priority** (top to bottom): 1. Doors (smaller targets, higher priority) 2. Windows 3. Section cuts 4. Columns 5. Fixtures 6. Railings 7. Beams 8. Walls 9. Stairs 10. Landings 11. Rooms (lowest priority) 12. Slabs 13. Annotations **Dragging Modes**: - **Marquee selection**: Drag to select multiple objects - **Move**: Drag selected object to new location - **Handle dragging**: Drag handles to modify shape (walls, section cuts, railings, slabs) - **Snap support**: When `snapEnabled`, walls snap to endpoints/intersections, doors/windows slide along parent walls **Keyboard Shortcuts** (in Select mode): - **Delete/Backspace**: Delete selected object - **Ctrl+D** (columns only): Duplicate column **Cursor feedback**: - Crosshair for marquee selection - Grabbing for active drag - Default otherwise ### 5. **Editor State Management** (`src/app/EditorProvider.jsx`) The EditorProvider manages interaction state via useReducer with these key states: ```javascript { activeTool: TOOLS.SELECT, modelTarget: 'floor' | 'roof' | 'truss', selectedId: string | null, selectedType: string | null, toolState: {} // handler-specific state (dragging, handles, etc.) workspaceMode: 'model' | 'sheet', viewport: { panX, panY, zoom }, modelViewport, sheetViewport, // separate for each mode showGrid: boolean, snapEnabled: boolean, viewMode: 'plan' | 'elevation_*' | 'section_view' | 'truss_detail', activeFloorId: string, activeSheetId: string, activeSectionCutId: string, statusMessage: string | null, // user feedback message regionSelection: { bounds, selection, objectCount }, pastePreview: { active, point }, maximizedPanel: 'preview' | 'canvas' | null, activePhaseId: string | null, phaseViewMode: 'all' | 'single' | 'cumulative', } ``` **Action Types** (dispatcher commands): - `SET_TOOL` - Change active tool - `SELECT_OBJECT` / `DESELECT` - Selection management - `SET_VIEW_MODE` - Switch between plan/elevation/section - `SET_MODEL_TARGET` - Switch floor/roof/truss - `UPDATE_TOOL_STATE` - Handler state updates - `SET_STATUS_MESSAGE` / `CLEAR_STATUS_MESSAGE` - User feedback - Various viewport/pan/zoom actions ### 6. **User Interface Components** **Toolbar** (`src/ui/Toolbar.jsx`): - Tool palette with 12 primary tools + fixtures submenu - Each tool button shows label + keyboard shortcut in tooltip - View mode segmented control (Plan, Front, Rear, Left, Right, Section) - Workspace mode buttons (Floor, Roof, Truss, Sheets) - Undo/Redo with keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+Z, Ctrl+Shift+Z) - Grid/Snap toggles with persistent state - Copy/Cut/Paste buttons with clipboard support - Room detection auto-tool - Keyboard shortcuts defined inline (e.g., "V" for Select, "W" for Wall) **Sidebar** (`src/ui/Sidebar.jsx`): - Collapsible sections for organization - Hierarchical listings: Floors, Roof, Trusses, Sheets, Phases - Floor cards show elevation in mm - Phase management with visibility toggling, reordering, deletion - Context-sensitive content (shows floor/roof details when selected) - Click to select/navigate to any object in the model **Properties Panel** (`src/ui/PropertiesPanel.jsx`): - Shows editable properties for selected object - Large component (28k+ lines) handling all object types - Unit selection (mm/m) with live conversion - Conditional rendering based on object type - Input validation and constraints ### 7. **Status Message Feedback** (`src/renderers/SvgCanvas.jsx`) Status messages are transient notifications displayed in the bottom status bar: ```javascript // Display logic {statusMessage && {statusMessage}} // Auto-clear after 2.5 seconds useEffect(() => { if (!statusMessage) return; const timer = window.setTimeout(() => { editorDispatch({ type: 'CLEAR_STATUS_MESSAGE' }); }, 2500); return () => window.clearTimeout(timer); }, [statusMessage, editorDispatch]); ``` **Status Messages Used**: - Undo/Redo confirmations: "Undid last change.", "Redid last change." - Room detection: "Detected N room(s)." or "No enclosed rooms detected." - Truss creation guidance: "Select two support beams to create a truss." - Error feedback: "Draw at least 3 walls first." - Paste mode: "Paste: click to place" (shown in status bar) ### 8. **Keyboard Shortcut Handling** **Global Shortcuts** (in Toolbar): - **Ctrl+Z** / **Ctrl+Shift+Z** - Undo/Redo - **Ctrl+C** / **Ctrl+X** / **Ctrl+V** - Copy/Cut/Paste - **Ctrl+S** - Save **Tool Shortcuts** (press key to activate): - **V** - Select - **M** - Dimension - **W** - Wall - **B** - Beam - **T** - Stair - **Q** - Section Cut - **S** - Slab - **R** - Room - **D** - Door - **N** - Window - **C** - Column - **L** - Landing - **H** - Railing - **F** - Fixture (opens palette) - **P** - Parapet (roof mode) - **G** - Drain (roof mode) - **O** - Opening (roof mode) **Context-Specific**: - **Delete** / **Backspace** - Delete selected - **Ctrl+D** - Duplicate column (when selected) - **Shift** - Constrain angle when drawing walls (snaps to 45° angles) - **Space** - Pan canvas (changes cursor to grab) Keyboard detection uses `isTypingTarget()` utility to prevent shortcuts when focused on input fields. ### 9. **Accessibility Features** - **aria-label** attributes on buttons (e.g., "Undo", "Redo", "Toggle Sidebar") - **aria-expanded** on collapsible sections - **role="group"** for button groups (phase view mode) - **title** attributes on all toolbar buttons for tooltip hints - Semantic HTML structure with proper ARIA roles ### 10. **No Built-in Tooltip/Popover System** The application relies on: - **Native HTML `title` attribute** - Shows OS tooltips on hover - **Status bar messages** - Transient feedback in `SvgCanvas` - **Aria-labels** - For screen readers - **Property Panel** - Context-sensitive details when objects selected There are no custom tooltip, popover, or notification components. Feedback is minimal and integrated into the status bar. ### 11. **Cursor Feedback States** - `'crosshair'` - Marquee selection mode - `'grabbing'` - Actively dragging - `'grab'` - Space bar held (pan mode) - `'copy'` - Paste preview active - `'default'` - Normal interaction - Handler-specific cursors may vary per tool ### File Paths Summary - **Tool definitions**: `/src/editor/tools.js` - **Tool hook/dispatcher**: `/src/editor/useEditorTool.js` - **Select handler**: `/src/editor/handlers/selectHandler.js` - **Editor state**: `/src/app/EditorProvider.jsx` - **Toolbar UI**: `/src/ui/Toolbar.jsx` - **Sidebar UI**: `/src/ui/Sidebar.jsx` - **Canvas (status display)**: `/src/renderers/SvgCanvas.jsx` - **Keyboard utilities**: `/src/utils/keyboard.js` - **All other handlers**: `/src/editor/handlers/*` This is a clean, handler-based architecture where tools are stateless factories that return interaction objects, state is centralized in Redux-like providers, and UI is declaratively composed from React components.
